response.justification: The candidate far exceeds the technical bar for this role: nearly two decades of fullstack experience, strong TypeScript/Node/React work at multiple Berlin companies, plus AWS, Kubernetes, and recent hands-on LLM/MCP tooling that covers every nice-to-have. However, this is an entry-level junior/mid position, and a Staff-level engineer currently employed applying to it raises serious questions about role fit, compensation alignment, motivation, and retention. Worth a conversation only if there is a credible reason for the level mismatch or flexibility to hire at a higher level.
strengths: Deep TypeScript/Node.js/React production experience directly matching the stack
Covers all nice-to-haves: LLM/MCP integration, AWS (CDK), Kubernetes, Docker
Strong engineering-culture signals: TDD, testing tools authorship, workshops, open source
concerns: Heavily overqualified for an entry-level role; likely compensation and retention mismatch
No explicit PostgreSQL experience (MongoDB/MySQL listed instead)
Only basic German, which may matter for a high on-site energy-consulting platform
